Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:32 ET
Quantum bubble meets institutional dispersion as call flow explodes while index puts surge
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Massive divergence with index puts at 1.86 P/C while single-name calls at 0.71 suggests institutional dispersion hedge positioning
  • 2. Quantum computing names see extreme call speculation with QUBT +999% volume and implied correlation at 0.03 enabling stock-picking
  • 3. AMZN May/June call blocks worth $1.1B signal major institutional bet ahead of potential AI infrastructure catalyst
NARRATIVE — greed with dispersion setup

The most striking signal is the extreme P/C divergence — index puts heavy at 1.86 while single-name calls surge at 0.71, a classic institutional dispersion setup where hedge funds buy individual stock calls while hedging with index puts. This 1.15-point spread reflects confidence in stock selection despite macro caution, amplified by implied correlation at 0.03 enabling efficient single-name vol strategies over index hedging. Quantum computing speculation reaches fever pitch with QUBT call volume exploding +999% and P/C at 0.03, while IONQ and CLSK see similar patterns amid continued quantum hardware breakthroughs and government funding announcements. The extreme call skew (six of eight top unusual flow names showing call-heavy action) coincides with 0.03 implied correlation — the lowest regime where individual stock vol trades most efficiently against index positioning. AMZN block activity stands out with $1.1B in May-June call spreads (37.5K contracts each in 260C/290C May, 25K in 265C June) despite the death cross technical pattern, suggesting institutional conviction around AI infrastructure acceleration or AWS growth acceleration ahead of quarterly results. The positioning conflicts with Millennium's massive Q4 reversal from reduction to 398% addition, indicating smart money rotation into cloud infrastructure plays.

Layer 1: Sentiment +52Layer 3: P/C divergence 1.86 vs 0.71Layer 4: IC 0.03Layer 3: AMZN blocks $1.1B
